accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and verified context; the rule, agency, citation, and dates are correct.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the CRA mechanism and DOE attribution are accurate context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the purpose is to nullify the DOE appliance standards rule via congressional disapproval.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly identifies it nullifies the specified DOE rule under Chapter 8 of Title 5 (Congressional Review Act).

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill is a simple disapproval resolution with no exceptions or carve-outs to note.

effective_dates.why: Correctly states it became effective upon approval on May 9, 2025, matching the bill.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the essential operative effect: the rule shall have no force or effect.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no editorializing.

concise.why: Appropriately brief with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Department of Energy, the rule title, and Federal Register citation are all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: All dates and citations (Oct 9, 2024; 89 Fed. Reg. 81994; May 9, 2025; Mar 5, 2025; Apr 30, 2025) are correct.
